title: 2015-16 Departmental Performance Report - Canada.ca
content:
## 2015-16 Departmental Performance Report

## Sources of Respendable Non-Tax Revenue and Non-Respendable Non-Tax Revenue
By virtue of its mandate, the CRA is responsible for collecting tax revenue as described earlier in the Audited Financial Statements – Administered Activities. The CRA also collects non-tax revenues which are further divided between respendable and non-respendable. Respendable non-tax revenues are received by the CRA for services provided to individuals or organizations that have not been funded by Parliament, for example, costs recovered under tax collection agreements with the provinces. Non-respendable non-tax revenues are those received by the CRA for which the CRA does not have the authority to re-spend, for example, recovery of employee benefits costs.
